Acts chapter 24 this evening, Acts chapter number 24, and be looking at a passage of scripture before we get into the text, I wanna remind you of what we've done here over the last six months. Over the last six months, starting on the first Wednesday of January of this calendar year and ending last Wednesday, the last Wednesday of June, we spent those six months examining 27 verses of scripture. Those 27 verses of scripture were found in 1 Thessalonians 5 and in 2 Thessalonians chapter 3. As we examine those portions of scripture, those 27 verses of scripture, we find that those verses of scripture give instruction for how a child of God should behave if they want to walk worthy and please God. I believe this evening that if I were to come and ask each of you individually, do you want to walk worthy, do you want to please God with your life, I think tonight that all of you would say yes. I desire to walk worthy, I desire to please God with my life, the problem is that just having a desire really isn't enough and we find that as we've studied through those portions of scripture and even in recent messages this Sunday morning, we stressed the importance of applying biblical principles in our lives, developing biblical perspective. So how is it that we take the Bible truth and apply that truth in my life, in your life, how is it that we can walk worthy and please God? And we find this evening it's not enough to simply read the Bible, it's not enough to simply hear the Bible, it's not even enough to just quote the Bible, the reality is that we must apply the truth of God's word in our lives. And I'm thankful this evening that the Bible has been given to us, but we do find that the Bible, it is not a book of suggestions, amen. Do you agree with that tonight? It's not a book of suggestions, we find that God's not suggesting that here's a better path, a better way, no, God is giving us the way, he's providing for us not a list of suggestions that we can come and say well I like that but I don't really like that, it's not for us to take some and leave some, it's not a buffet type of a situation. And we find that God has given us his word and it is imperative that we accept all of the Bible. Not just the parts that we might say well I like that, I like how that kinda, I can fit that in my life, no, all of scripture, we rejoice with rehearsing verses like 2 Timothy 3 and verse number 16 when the Bible says all scripture is given by inspiration of God and we rejoice in the fact that that word all is there because he doesn't just say scripture is given by inspiration but he says all scripture is given by inspiration of God and so we can rejoice in that, we look over in the book of Psalms and the book of Proverbs and we read passages of scripture in both of those books that deal with the subject matter of preservation and how God has providentially preserved his word and we find that the book of Proverbs tells us that every word of God is pure, every word. And I'm thankful for every word of God, I'm thankful that God gave us exactly what he knew that we needed. I'm thankful that God has given us the Bible and we must believe every word from Genesis 1-1 to Revelation 22-21, the entirety, the totality of scripture. We find that the accusers are gathered, we find here in this text of scripture that there are these according to verse two that have come to accuse him, they say it again at the end of verse number eight, whereof we accuse him. So the accusers are gathered, the accusation is laid out, you find it in verse five and six, the accusation that has brought the charge if you will against Paul but we find that Paul after hearing these opening arguments if you will, as he's standing there to give defense before Felix, we find that Paul makes an interesting statement, he says at the end of verse number 10, I do the more cheerfully answer for myself, the apostle Paul was so thankful that he had an opportunity to give an answer. The accusers were there, the accusation was laid out but Paul says I'm rejoicing because I get to answer for myself, well within his answer, Paul reveals a truth that is important for each of us tonight especially with regard to what we've dealt with over these last six months of walk worthy, please God. We've looked at these 27 verses of scripture, we've dealt with the principles that are given to God's people, the church at Thessalonica being the original recipients but every local church from that time until now that they have the preserved word of God and God says here's my instruction, here's the truth on how you can walk worthy and please me.
